all.en: When I came home for the long weekend, the tension between my parents was hanging in the air.
all.zh: 當我回家過長週末時，父母之間的緊張氣氛瀰漫在空氣中。

all.en: Mom was slamming cabinet doors in the kitchen while Dad pretended to read a financial magazine.
all.zh: 媽媽在廚房裡大力關著櫥櫃門，爸爸則假裝在讀一本財經雜誌。

all.en: Neither of them looked up when I walked in with my suitcase and a bag of pineapple cakes.
all.zh: 當我提著行李箱和一袋鳳梨酥走進門時，他們兩個誰也沒抬頭看我。

all.en: My younger brother whispered to me that they had been arguing about retirement plans for three weeks.
all.zh: 弟弟悄悄告訴我，他們為了退休計畫已經吵了三個星期了。

all.en: Dad wanted to sell our family house in Taipei and move back to his hometown in Yilan.
all.zh: 爸爸想把我們在台北的房子賣掉，搬回他在宜蘭的老家。

all.en: Mom, however, refused to leave the city where her friends, her hairdresser, and her doctors were.
all.zh: 然而媽媽拒絕離開這個有她朋友、髮型師和醫生的城市。

all.en: Over dinner, every neutral topic somehow turned into a sharp remark about the other person's stubbornness.
all.zh: 晚餐時，每個原本中立的話題都會莫名其妙地變成對對方固執的尖銳批評。

all.en: I decided I could not let this dinner end with another fight over leftover stir-fried cabbage.
all.zh: 我決定不能讓這頓飯又因為一盤剩下的炒高麗菜而以爭吵收場。

all.en: After cleaning the dishes, I asked both of them to sit down with me in the living room.
all.zh: 洗完碗後，我請他們兩個都到客廳和我坐下來。

all.en: I told them I was not taking sides but I needed to understand what each of them really wanted.
all.zh: 我告訴他們我不會偏袒任何一方，但我需要了解他們真正想要的是什麼。

all.en: Dad explained that he missed the smell of rice fields and the slow pace of country life.
all.zh: 爸爸解釋說他想念稻田的氣味，還有鄉下緩慢的生活步調。

all.en: He also worried about the rising property taxes on our aging Taipei apartment building.
all.zh: 他也擔心我們那棟老舊台北公寓越來越高的房屋稅。

all.en: Mom said she was terrified of losing the social circle she had built over the past thirty years.
all.zh: 媽媽說她非常害怕失去過去三十年所建立起來的社交圈。

all.en: Without her weekly tai chi group and book club, she felt she might lose her sense of self.
all.zh: 沒有了每週的太極拳社團和讀書會，她覺得自己可能會失去自我。

all.en: It struck me that both of them were really afraid of the same thing, only in different shapes.
all.zh: 我突然發現他們其實都在害怕同樣的事，只是表現的方式不同。

all.en: They were both afraid that the next chapter of their lives would feel lonely and unfamiliar.
all.zh: 他們都害怕人生的下一個階段會讓他們覺得孤單而陌生。

all.en: I suggested a compromise neither of them had considered during their previous heated arguments.
all.zh: 我提出了一個折衷方案，是他們在之前激烈爭吵中都沒有想過的。

all.en: What if they kept a small apartment in Taipei but bought a modest house in Yilan as well?
all.zh: 如果他們在台北留下一間小公寓，同時在宜蘭也買一間樸實的房子呢？

all.en: Mom frowned and immediately said they could never afford two homes on their current savings.
all.zh: 媽媽皺著眉頭，立刻說以他們現在的存款根本買不起兩個家。

all.en: I had already done some math on the train ride home and pulled out my notebook.
all.zh: 我在回家的火車上已經算過一些數字，便拿出了我的筆記本。

all.en: By renting out the Taipei apartment during the week, they could cover most of the country mortgage.
all.zh: 只要平日把台北的公寓出租，他們就能負擔大部分鄉下的房貸。

all.en: Dad's eyes lit up at the idea of becoming a small-scale landlord in his old neighborhood.
all.zh: 爸爸一聽到要在老社區當個小房東就眼睛一亮。

all.en: Mom was still suspicious, so I encouraged her to call her best friend, Auntie Lin, for advice.
all.zh: 媽媽仍然心存懷疑，所以我鼓勵她打電話給她最好的朋友林阿姨徵詢意見。

all.en: Auntie Lin reminded her that high-speed rail makes Yilan only an hour away from her social life.
all.zh: 林阿姨提醒她，搭高鐵到宜蘭距離她的社交生活也只有一小時。

all.en: By Sunday morning, my parents were sitting at the table looking at real estate listings together.
all.zh: 到了星期天早上，我的父母已經坐在桌前一起翻看房地產的資料。

all.en: Dad even apologized for calling Mom selfish during their fight on Wednesday night.
all.zh: 爸爸甚至為了週三晚上吵架時罵媽媽自私而道歉。

all.en: Mom in return admitted she had been dismissive of his dream to grow vegetables again.
all.zh: 媽媽則承認她一直忽略了爸爸想要再次種菜的夢想。

all.en: My brother, who had been quietly watching, finally joined the conversation with his own questions.
all.zh: 一直在旁靜靜觀察的弟弟，這時終於開口加入了討論。

all.en: He worried about where we would all gather for Lunar New Year if there were two homes.
all.zh: 他擔心如果有兩個家，我們全家過年要在哪裡團聚。

all.en: Mom hugged him and promised that Lunar New Year would always be held wherever she was cooking.
all.zh: 媽媽抱住他，承諾無論她在哪裡做菜，過年就一定會在那裡。

all.en: Before I left for the train station, Dad pulled me aside for a quiet, serious chat.
all.zh: 在我前往火車站之前，爸爸把我拉到一旁進行了一場安靜而嚴肅的談話。

all.en: He thanked me for treating them like real people rather than just my parents.
all.zh: 他感謝我把他們當成真正的人來對待，而不只是當成我的父母。

all.en: On the train, I realized my role in the family had quietly shifted over the past few years.
all.zh: 在火車上，我發現自己在家庭中的角色在過去幾年裡已經悄悄改變了。

all.en: I was no longer just their daughter; I was also a bridge between two stubborn people I loved.
all.zh: 我不再只是他們的女兒，更是我所愛的兩個固執的人之間的一座橋樑。

all.en: When my brother texted to say they were laughing together over coffee, I finally let myself relax.
all.zh: 當弟弟傳簡訊告訴我他們正一起喝咖啡有說有笑時，我終於讓自己放鬆下來。

all.en: Two weeks later, my parents made their first scouting trip together down to coastal Yilan.
all.zh: 兩週後，我的父母第一次一起到沿海的宜蘭實地探勘。

all.en: They photographed five different houses and sent me a long voice message about each one.
all.zh: 他們拍下了五間不同房子的照片，並為每一間錄了一段長長的語音訊息給我。

all.en: Mom was charmed by a small garden with a lemon tree right beside the kitchen window.
all.zh: 媽媽被一個就在廚房窗邊有檸檬樹的小花園深深吸引。

all.en: Dad fell in love with a wooden porch where he imagined drinking tea on summer evenings.
all.zh: 爸爸愛上了一個木造的門廊，他想像著在夏夜在那裡喝茶。

all.en: By the third visit, they had signed a contract on a modest cottage near the train station.
all.zh: 到了第三次造訪，他們已經在火車站附近簽下了一間樸實的小屋契約。

all.en: Auntie Lin promised to visit at least one weekend a month with a thermos of strong oolong tea.
all.zh: 林阿姨答應每個月至少有一個週末會帶著一壺濃烈的烏龍茶來拜訪。

all.en: The Taipei apartment found two careful young tenants within a single week of being listed.
all.zh: 台北的公寓在刊登後一週內就找到了兩位細心的年輕房客。

all.en: On moving day, my brother and I drove down to help carry boxes and arrange furniture.
all.zh: 搬家當天，我和弟弟開車南下幫忙搬箱子和擺家具。

all.en: Dad planted two tomato seedlings in the garden before we had even finished unpacking the kitchen.
all.zh: 爸爸在我們連廚房都還沒收拾完之前，就在花園裡種下了兩株番茄苗。

all.en: That evening, all four of us sat on the porch watching fireflies and feeling completely at home.
all.zh: 那天傍晚，我們四個人坐在門廊上看著螢火蟲，感到無比安心自在。

all.en: Within six months, Dad's small vegetable garden was producing more tomatoes than the family could eat.
all.zh: 六個月內，爸爸的小菜園產出的番茄就多到全家吃不完。

all.en: He started giving baskets of produce to elderly neighbors and quickly became known as a kind grower.
all.zh: 他開始把一籃籃蔬菜送給年長的鄰居，很快就被稱讚為一位親切的菜農。

all.en: Mom joined a community choir in Yilan and discovered she had a surprisingly strong alto voice.
all.zh: 媽媽在宜蘭加入了一個社區合唱團，發現自己竟然有相當有力的女低音嗓音。

all.en: She also started taking the train back to Taipei twice a month for her tai chi sessions.
all.zh: 她也開始每個月搭兩次火車回台北參加她的太極拳練習。

all.en: The Taipei tenants turned out to be careful young librarians who watered Mom's old houseplants weekly.
all.zh: 台北的房客是兩位細心的年輕圖書館員，每週都會幫媽媽的老盆栽澆水。

all.en: My brother and I began alternating weekends to visit, bringing snacks and gossip from the city.
all.zh: 我和弟弟開始輪流在週末造訪，帶著城裡的零食和八卦過去。

all.en: On rainy nights, the four of us played mahjong while listening to old Taiwanese pop songs.
all.zh: 雨夜時，我們四個人會一邊打麻將一邊聽老台灣流行歌。

all.en: Dad eventually built a wooden swing on the porch where Mom liked to read every afternoon.
all.zh: 爸爸後來在門廊上做了一個木鞦韆，媽媽喜歡每天下午在那裡看書。

all.en: Auntie Lin began bringing different friends along, slowly building a small Taipei circle in Yilan.
all.zh: 林阿姨開始帶不同的朋友一起來，慢慢在宜蘭建立起一個小型台北朋友圈。

all.en: One year later, my parents told me their retirement had become the happiest chapter of their lives.
all.zh: 一年之後，父母告訴我，他們的退休生活已成為人生中最幸福的篇章。
